name: The Grand Illusion
flavor: Every eye in the tent turns to you at once. What they see next, they will never fully stop seeing.
components: Prop (a folding fan or opera mask)
description: You spend a surge of banked Phantasm to seize the minds of every creature you choose within a 30-foot-radius sphere centred on a point within range, showing each of them their own private, tailored nightmare all at once. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your {{trick save DC|8 + proficiency bonus + your trick ability modifier}}. On a failure, a target takes [[5d6]] psychic damage and is frightened of you for the duration; on a success, it takes half as much damage and is not frightened. A frightened target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the frightened condition on itself on a success. This trick requires you to have at least 4 Phantasm banked, which are spent whether or not any target fails its save.
sheetSummary: Once per combat. Action, 90 ft, 30-ft-radius sphere, costs 4 Phantasm, concentration up to 1 min. Wisdom save vs trick save DC per target. Fail: [[5d6]] psychic damage, frightened for duration (repeat save at end of each turn). Success: half damage, no condition.
